title: Common content used by multiple parameter schemas (reduces copy & paste)

noteDesc.description: A non-normative note about the normative rule

descriptionDesc.description: Comprehensive description of the normative rule (sentence, paragraph, or longer)

implDefBehaviorDesc.description: A normative rule that allows an implementation choices in how to meet the rule

paramBehaviorDesc.description: A parameter that maps to one or more implementation-defined behaviors in the standards documentation

paramTypeDesc.description: Can be boolean, bit, byte, hword, word, dword, int, uint, array of strings, or array of integers. Use "range" for min/max ranges. Specify width property for int/uint

paramRangeDesc.description: Array of 2 integers specifying lower and upper bound (in that order)

paramWidthDesc.description: Either an integer bit width between 2 and 64 (inclusive) or the name of another parameter that provides the bit width. Used only with type "int" or "uint"

paramRange.description: Inclusive range [lo, hi] as two integers

paramArray.description: Inclusive index bounds [lo, hi] as two non-negative integers

legal.description: Array of one or more legal values (decimal integer, 0x hex, or 0b binary)

illegal-write-ignore.description: Must be true; indicates illegal writes are silently ignored

illegal-write-return.description: If specified, illegal writes return this value (decimal integer, 0x hex, or 0b binary)

legal.description: Array of one or more legal integers (converted to decimal)

illegal-write-ignore.description: Must be true; indicates illegal writes are silently ignored

illegal-write-return.description: Value returned on illegal writes (converted to decimal)
